# Cybersecurity Club of Softwarica

> Hack to Learn, Learn to Defend — a student-driven community mastering ethical hacking, CTF competitions, and defensive security at Softwarica College of IT & E-Commerce, Nepal.

## About

The Cybersecurity Club of Softwarica (also known as 26 Club) is a student-run organization at Softwarica College of IT & E-Commerce in Nepal. The club focuses on building practical cybersecurity skills through hands-on learning, ethical hacking practice, Capture the Flag (CTF) competitions, cryptography, and defensive security training.

## Topics

- Ethical Hacking
- Capture the Flag (CTF) Competitions
- Defensive Security
- Cryptography
- Network Security
- Web Application Security
- Penetration Testing
- Security Research

## Pages

- Homepage: /
- Terms of Service: /terms-of-service

## Terms of Service

The club operates under a formal code of conduct covering membership eligibility (enrolled Softwarica students in good standing), behavioral standards (ethical, authorized use only), governance by a Board of Directors (BoDs) with faculty advisor support, a progressive disciplinary process, and an annual review cycle.
